title: SharePoint Online DLP Policy Matches (Office 365 DLP Feed)
dataSources: `sourcetype=ms:o365:dlp` events with SharePoint or OneDrive workload context
spl:
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:dlp" earliest=-24h
| eval wl=lower(coalesce(Workload, workload, ""))
| where match(wl, "sharepoint|onedrive")
| eval policy=coalesce(PolicyName, policyName, Workload, "unknown")
| eval actor=coalesce(UserId, UserPrincipalName, userId, "")
| stats count by policy, actor, Severity
| sort -count
description: DLP events scoped to SharePoint or OneDrive workloads show policy violations where sensitive labels, PII, or IP leave structured collaboration repositories.
value: Bridges email-centric DLP monitoring into modern file collaboration, helping teams respond before synchronized clients spread violations broadly.
implementation: Confirm `Workload` and `Severity` fields exist in your DLP feed; some tenants require `spath` on JSON payloads. Map `PolicyName` to data-classification registers. Route critical severities to the same queue as endpoint DLP alerts.
visualization: Bar chart (policy × severity), table (actor × policy), timeline.
knownFalsePositives: Office 365 DLP test data, simulation rules, and site templates that still carry sensitive sample files.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable; primary SPL uses raw or vendor-normalized event fields.
grandmaExplanation: We see when Google’s rules block or flag sensitive data, so teams can tune what is really sensitive versus noisy.
